title: Same-Lap Query Ranker Expert
summary: Inference from XGBoost/CatBoost ranking docs: this task is partly relative within each (Year, Race, LapNumber) field, where teams choose who is most likely to pit among contemporaries.
rationale: Inference from XGBoost/CatBoost ranking docs: this task is partly relative within each (Year, Race, LapNumber) field, where teams choose who is most likely to pit among contemporaries. A querywise ranker can optimize within-lap ordering directly instead of only global ROC ranking.
implementation_hint: Train one extra expert with qid=(Year,Race,LapNumber), using XGBoost `rank:pairwise`/`rank:ndcg` or CatBoost `QuerySoftMax`/`YetiRankPairwise`. Keep the current classifier stack, then blend the ranker score as another OOF meta-feature. Filter or downweight queries with no positive labels.
expected_effect: Better separation among near-threshold cars on the same lap; likely most useful for public-LB robustness when race-level ordering matters more than absolute calibration.
risk: Many lap-groups may have zero or one positive, so ranking gradients can be sparse; naive random folds can also leak query structure and overstate gains.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
